entities.name: STORAGE LIFE
entities.description: The duration for which onion bulbs remain viable and marketable

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL LOSS IN WEIGHT
entities.description: Reduction in weight of onion bulbs during storage

entities.name: ROTTING
entities.description: Decay of onion bulbs during storage

entities.name: SPROUTING
entities.description: Growth of sprouts from onion bulbs during storage

entities.name: MARKETABLE BULBS
entities.description: The percentage or yield of bulbs suitable for sale

entities.name: POTASSIUM LEVELS
entities.description: Different dosages of potassium, specifically 100%, 175%, and 200% RDK

entities.name: POTASSIUM SOURCES
entities.description: Different forms of potassium fertilizer used in the study

entities.name: TIME OF APPLICATION
entities.description: The timing of potassium application, such as at transplanting and 30 DAT

entities.name: SOP
entities.description: Potassium sulphate, a specific source of potassium

entities.name: MOP
entities.description: Muriate of potash, a specific source of potassium

entities.name: BAGALKOT, KARNATAKA
entities.description: The location of the College of Horticulture where the study was conducted

entities.name: KHARIF SEASON
entities.description: The agricultural season of 2015 and 2016 during which the crop was grown
